title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering A

prerequisites.text: Refer to the programme and departmental sections in the 2026 EBE Faculty Handbook.

outline: This course aims to motivate and help students understand the nature and scope of electronic engineering by providing an introduction to the content, methods and modes of thinking. A further aim is to develop students’ confidence in rational problem -solving approaches and to introduce students to the design process. Topics include: basic concepts, basic laws, methods of analysis, circuit theorems, properties of basic components and first order circuits.
lecture_times: See departmental timetable and official class schedule.
dp_requirements: Please refer to the official course handout document for detailed information regarding the DP requirements for this course.
assessment: Please refer to the official course handout document regarding the assessment criteria for this course.
